label: wear | fashion | outfit | buy | outfits | trends
examples:
🗣️ for me myself, i don’t buy shein, but i do buy forever 21. but i don’t follow fashion trends. i have an alt clothing style. so, that means that even thought i do shop at forever 21, h&m, hot topic, etc, i don’t get rid of my clothes as soon as they’re out of style. because i don’t follow normal fashion trends, i’ve had lots of the same clothes for years and years, and i resuse them, wash them, and make new outfits. i really hate how people think to be glamorous, you have to have different outfits every day. you dont ^^ reuse shirts and pants to make new different combinations. reuse jewelry to make even better jewelry matching. and most important, wear what you want to wear. don’t follow fashion trends if you don’t want to. wear what makes you feel comfortable, and if people hate you for wearing pieces of fabric that’s different from theirs, who cares! it’s your body, your decision

🗣️ as someone who works in the fashion industry it is a challenge to appear on trend, yet having a heart that says i don’t need new clothes. i have a closet full of clothes that are beautiful, good quality, fit well but i still have trouble wearing the same outfits over and over. normalize repeating outfits!!

🗣️ guys, we need to normalise re-wearing clothes and outfit repeating. it is literally the only way to solve all the problems that fast fashion perpetuate (overconsumption and needless waste, pollution, shitty quality clothes, sweatshops). i know we all like the dopamine hit we get when we buy new things (especially when it's a bargain). i know we like it when people compliment us when we are wearing something new and pretty. but seriously, living this way is destroying our environment and promoting the exploitation of people (mostly women and children) in developing countries. so guys, wherever possible, buy secondhand. and if you must buy brand new, cherish the item! look after it, wear it multiple times until it gets holes, then stitch up the holes and wear the item multiple more times!

label: buy | thrift | clothing | fashion | wear | cheap
examples:
🗣️ i’m southeast asian and even the cheapest shein clothes still quite expensive for me so i’ve never buy one. i rarely buy new clothes, when i do i bought them from thrifted and will only buy one if it’s around $3 or under $6 (though in my country, thrifting has become sooo well known that sellers can charge for more than $10 for a single used clothes). all those fast fashion brand such as h&m, zara, forever21 and uniqlo to me is an expensive brand. i only have 1 or 2 clothes with that brand and i treasure them so much and i use it for special occasions. this is so sad that those are actually cheap clothes and they throw away hundred clothes meanwhile in another side of world, there’s someone who actually treasure the brand with thoughts it’s an expensive brand.

🗣️ i'm in the sort of weird "stuck between a rock and a hard place" clothing sphere. i buy cheap clothes because that's all i can afford. i am aware of how bad it is for the people who make it and the environment, however i have no choice in the matter due to constant financial struggles. while i buy cheap, i also buy seldom. last time i bought clothes was 2020. while i participate in what you would call "fast fashion" i actually keep my clothes for years and years until they cant be publicly presentable anymore, at which point they become at home clothes until they get so damaged that repairing them just isn't worth the hassle.

🗣️ some people such as myself shop from shein out of necessity. i have $150 for the whole season, not for a couple of pieces. ethical fashion is very out of reach for me. yes i shop thrift stores but honestly thrift stores are increasing picked through by resellers and i end up feeling not put together when my whole outfit is thrifted. plus a thrifted wardrobe is hard to make feel cohesive and complete, i always feel slightly messy. i just bought four viscose maxi dresses and four cotton long sleeve tops for $89 and i love them and wear them every day, i have a minimal capsule wardrobe and will wear these pieces until they fall apart. i think a lot of people who look down on fast fashion are in positions of privilege and have extra income to allocate towards fashion. i shop as responsibly as i can afford to and i am minimal in my purchases. i also hand wash all of my clothing to extend their lives. just another perspective
summary: i'm in the sort of weird "stuck between a rock and a hard place" clothing sphere . i buy cheap clothes because that's all i can afford . i'm southeast asian and even the cheapest shein clothes still quite expensive for me so i’ve never bought
